accurate.why: All claims accurately reflect the bill text and correct contextual information about the Home Rule Act review process.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or incorrect claims; the description of the disapproval mechanism is accurate real-world context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies that the resolution disapproves the D.C. Revised Criminal Code Act of 2022.

changes_to_existing_law.why: A disapproval resolution does not amend or repeal existing federal law; it simply nullifies the D.C. act.

exceptions_conditions.why: No material exceptions or conditions in this simple disapproval resolution.

effective_dates.why: Notes passage dates (Feb. 9, Mar. 8, 2023) and approval date (Mar. 20, 2023).

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the single operative provision — congressional disapproval blocking the D.C. act — fully.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and factual throughout; no advocacy or editorializing.

concise.why: Somewhat verbose; the last paragraph restating there are no conditions is unnecessary padding.

correct_entities.why: D.C. Council, D.C. Home Rule Act section 602(c)(1), D.C. Act 24-789 all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: All dates stated (Jan. 17, Jan. 27, Feb. 9, Mar. 8, Mar. 20, 2023) match the bill text.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
